leadText: Baxcalibur (Japanese: セグレイブ Seglaive) is a dual-type Dragon/Ice Pokémon introduced in Generation IX. It evolves from Arctibax starting at level 54. It is the final form of Frigibax. Baxcalibur can Mega Evolve into Mega Baxcalibur using the Baxcalibrite. Mega Baxcalibur was introduced with the Mega Dimension DLC for Pokémon Legends: Z-A. It is referred to as a pseudo-legendary Pokémon by fans.

sections.title: Biology
sections.text: Biology [ edit | edit source ] Baxcalibur is a large, theropod -like Pokémon whose body is predominantly navy blue in color. It has a series of hexagon-shaped ice patches located near its chest area, on its kneecaps, and underneath its tail. It also has squarish polygonal light blue patches underneath its feet. It has long muscular arms and three sharp white claws on its hands and feet, along with three red quills protruding from each of its wrists. It appears to be wearing an icy mask on its face, with a pair of ice spikes pointing upward on its snout and icicles pointing downward on its chin. The only part of the face not covered in ice is its yellow eyes. The black, ice-coated sail on its back resembles the blade of an axe or polearm. Baxcalibur can spew cryogenic air from its mouth, which is cold enough to freeze liquid-hot lava. Baxcalibur uses its icy, cold breath to freeze foes and pulverizes them with a blow from its tail. In battle, Baxcalibur flips upside-down while supporting itself with its head and forelimbs, and uses its frigid breath to launch itself sail-first towards its opponents, finishing them off. Baxcalibur is the only known Pokémon capable of learning the move Glaive Rush . Baxcalibur and its pre-evolved forms are also the only known Pokémon that can have Thermal Exchange as an Ability . Forms [ edit | edit source ] Mega Baxcalibur flipping upside-down for attack Baxcalibur can Mega Evolve into Mega Baxcalibur. The axe-like sail on Mega Baxcalibur's back has expanded and fused with an icy growth on the front of its stomach, specifically around the solar plexus area. This icy column formation resembles the hilt of a sword, thanks to the energy from Mega Evolution. [ 1 ] The sail now more closely resembles a large sword, with its frosted edges taking on a slight bluish gradient. The same goes for Mega Baxcalibur's mask, now wrapping around its eyes and the back of its head with additional hexagon-shaped ice patches. More of these ice patches add to its belly armor, with one patch separate from the main plating on each side, closer to Mega Baxcalibur's neck. Due to Mega Evolution, Mega Baxcalibur can generate significantly more ice energy than before. Mega Baxcalibur channels its ice energy through its solar plexus nerve, enhancing its abilities. This upgraded nerve helps stabilize its center of gravity, supporting the enlarged sail of its giant sword. Mega Baxcalibur can absorb ice energy from its hilt at the solar plexus and unleash beams from it. Thanks to Mega Evolution, ice energy permeates its entire body, impacting its internal organs and nerves. Its reaction speed and muscle agility are enhanced, increasing the power of its attacks and explosive movements. As a result, Mega Baxcalibur is more powerful than the standard version of itself. The redness of its pupils indicates that the ice energy has also affected its optic nerves. Mega Baxcalibur can use its larger blade's immense weight to cut through nearly anything in half or smash it to pieces. [ 1 ] Curiously, the "hilt" of Mega Baxcalibur's blade seems to resemble the symbol for Terastallization . Mega Baxcalibur Evolution [ edit | edit source ] Baxcalibur evolves from Arctibax and is the final form of Frigibax . (For specifics on this Pokémon's Evolution in the games, refer to Game data→Evolution data .) Unevolved Frigibax Dragon Ice → First Evolution Arctibax Dragon Ice → Second Evolution Baxcalibur Dragon Ice ↑ 1.0 1.1 Mega Dimension | Mega Baxcalibur | Pokémon Legends: Z‑A – Mega Dimension

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Baxcalibur and its pre-evolved forms are the only Ice-type Pokémon in the Dragon Egg Group . Baxcalibur is the only pseudo-legendary Pokémon whose catch rate differs across its entire evolutionary line. Baxcalibur has the highest base HP and Attack stats of all pseudo-legendary Pokémon . Furthermore, Baxcalibur has the lowest base Special Attack stat of all pseudo-legendary Pokémon. Mega Baxcalibur has the highest Attack stat of all Ice-type Pokémon, and is tied with Black and White Kyurem for the highest base stat total of all Ice-type Pokémon. Origin [ edit | edit source ] Baxcalibur may draw inspiration from kaiju . Its upright posture may allude to how most kaiju are humanoid in shape, due to the need to fit a human actor inside the creature suit in kaiju films. It also has traits of certain species of dinosaurs, most notably Concavenator , a theropod dinosaur which had a crest on its back and quills on its forelimbs. Fossils of the genus were first discovered in Spain , and the crest is hypothesized to have functioned as a tool of thermoregulation . Baxcalibur may also be based on other sail-backed prehistoric animals, such as Spinosaurus , Ouranosaurus , Arizonasaurus , and Dimetrodon . The diamond shape of its sail may allude to the plates on the back of stegosaurids . Its overall design may also reference old depictions of non-avian theropod dinosaurs in paleoart . Based on Baxcalibur's Japanese name and the way it utilizes its sail through the move Glaive Rush , this sail may be inspired by the head of a glaive . On Mega Baxcalibur, the sail combined with the growth on its stomach resembles a sword instead. Baxcalibur evolving at level 54 could be a reference to 1954, the year the first Godzilla movie was released. Name origin [ edit | edit source ] Baxcalibur may be a combination of back and Excalibur (mythical sword belonging to King Arthur ). Seglaive may be a combination of 背 se (back) and glaive (referring to a polearm weapon with origins in Europe, or short swords such as the Gladius ).
